name: django-denorm-iplweb
documentation: django-denorm-iplweb is a Django application to provide automatic management of denormalized database fields.

modules.name: denorm.helpers
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: denorm.helpers.find_fks
documentation:
Finds all ForeignKeys on 'from_model' pointing to 'to_model'.
If 'fk_name' is given only ForeignKeys matching that name are returned.

functions.name: denorm.helpers.find_m2ms
documentation:
Finds all ManyToManyFields on 'from_model' pointing to 'to_model'.
If 'm2m_name' is given only ManyToManyFields matching that name are returned.

functions.name: denorm.fields.denormalized
documentation:
Turns a callable into model field, analogous to python's `@property` decorator.
The callable will be used to compute the value of the field every time the model
gets saved.
If the callable has dependency information attached to it the fields value will
also be recomputed if the dependencies require it.

**Arguments:**

DBField (required)

: The type of field you want to use to save the data.
  Note that you have to use the field class and not an instance
  of it.

\*args, \*\*kwargs:

: Those will be passed unaltered into the constructor of `DBField`
  once it gets actually created.

methods.name: denorm.fields.AggregateField.get_denorm
documentation: Returns denorm instance

methods.name: denorm.fields.AggregateField.__init__
documentation:
**Arguments:**

manager_name:

: The name of the related manager to be counted.

filter:

: Filter, which is applied to manager. For example:

```pycon
>>> active_item_count = CountField('item_set', filter={'active__exact':True})
>>> adult_user_count = CountField('user_set', filter={'age__gt':18})
```

exclude:

: Do not include filter in aggregation

Any additional arguments are passed on to the contructor of
PositiveIntegerField.

methods.name: denorm.fields.AggregateField.pre_save
documentation:
Makes sure we never overwrite the count with an outdated value.
This is necessary because if the count was changed by
a trigger after this model instance was created, the value
we would write has not been updated.

classes.name: denorm.fields.CountField

methods.name: denorm.fields.CountField.__init__
documentation:
**Arguments:**

manager_name:

: The name of the related manager to be counted.

filter:

: Filter, which is applied to manager. For example:

```pycon
>>> active_item_count = CountField('item_set', filter={'active__exact':True})
>>> adult_user_count = CountField('user_set', filter={'age__gt':18})
```

Any additional arguments are passed on to the contructor of
PositiveIntegerField.

A `PositiveIntegerField` that stores the number of rows
related to this model instance through the specified manager.
The value will be incrementally updated when related objects
are added and removed.

classes.name: denorm.fields.SumField
documentation:
A `PositiveIntegerField` that stores sub of related field values
to this model instance through the specified manager.
The value will be incrementally updated when related objects
are added and removed.

classes.name: denorm.fields.CacheKeyField

methods.name: denorm.fields.CacheKeyField.__init__
documentation:
All arguments are passed on to the contructor of
BigIntegerField.

methods.name: denorm.fields.CacheKeyField.depend_on_related
documentation:
Add dependency information to the CacheKeyField.
Accepts the same arguments like the *denorm.depend_on_related* decorator

A `BigIntegerField` that gets set to a random value anytime
the model is saved or a dependency is triggered.
The field gets updated immediately and does not require *denorm.flush()*.
It currently cannot detect a direct (bulk)update to the model
it is declared in.

denorm.models
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: denorm.models.DirtyInstanceManager.mark_timeouts_as_failed
documentation: Housekeeping: mark as timed out, default: past 5 minutes

classes.name: denorm.models.DirtyInstance

methods.name: denorm.models.DirtyInstance.content_object_for_update
documentation:
Returns a self.content_object, only locked for update. Needs
to run inside a transaciton. Can return None because nowait=True

methods.name: denorm.models.DirtyInstance.find_similar
documentation:
Find similar objects to this one. Same content_type, same object_id; func_name if this
object has func_name, but in case of no func name -- find all objects, as no func name
means even broader scope:

methods.name: denorm.models.DirtyInstance.delete_similar
documentation: Remove similar DirtyInstances from db, which we haven't yet processed

Holds a reference to a model instance that may contain inconsistent data
that needs to be recalculated.
DirtyInstance instances are created by the insert/update/delete triggers
when related objects change.

functions.name: denorm.denorms.many_to_many_pre_save
documentation: Updates denormalised many-to-many fields for the model

functions.name: denorm.denorms.get_alldenorms
documentation: Get all denormalizations.

functions.name: denorm.denorms.rebuildall
documentation: Updates all models containing denormalized fields.

functions.name: denorm.denorms.install_triggers
documentation: Installs all required triggers in the database

functions.name: denorm.denorms.flush
documentation:
Updates all model instances marked as dirty by the DirtyInstance
model.
After this method finishes the DirtyInstance table is empty and
all denormalized fields have consistent data.

methods.name: denorm.denorms.Denorm.setup
documentation:
Adds 'self' to the global denorm list
and connects all needed signals.

methods.name: denorm.denorms.Denorm.update
documentation: Updates the denormalizations in all instances in the queryset 'qs'.

documentation: Calls setup() on all DenormDependency resolvers

methods.name: denorm.denorms.BaseCallbackDenorm.get_triggers
documentation:
Creates a list of all triggers needed to keep track of changes
to fields this denorm depends on.

Handles the denormalization of one field, using a python function
as a callback.

classes.name: denorm.denorms.CallbackDenorm
documentation: As above, but with extra triggers on self as described below

to fields this denorm depends on.

methods.name: denorm.denorms.TriggerWhereNode.sql_for_columns
documentation:
Returns the SQL fragment used for the left-hand side of a column
constraint (for example, the "T1.foo" portion in the clause
"WHERE ... T1.foo = 6").

methods.name: denorm.denorms.AggregateDenorm.m2m_triggers
documentation: Returns triggers for m2m relation

methods.name: denorm.denorms.AggregateDenorm.get_increment_value
documentation: Returns SQL for incrementing value

methods.name: denorm.denorms.AggregateDenorm.get_decrement_value
documentation: Returns SQL for decrementing value

classes.name: denorm.denorms.SumDenorm
documentation: Handles denormalization of a sum field by doing incrementally updates.

classes.name: denorm.denorms.CountDenorm
documentation:
Handles the denormalization of a count field by doing incrementally
updates.

modules.name: denorm.dependencies
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: denorm.dependencies.make_depend_decorator
documentation:
Create a decorator that attaches an instance of the given class
to the decorated function, passing all remaining arguments to the classes
__init__.

classes.name: denorm.dependencies.DenormDependency

methods.name: denorm.dependencies.DenormDependency.get_triggers
documentation: Must return a list of `denorm.triggers.Trigger` instances

methods.name: denorm.dependencies.DenormDependency.setup
documentation: Remembers the model this dependency was declared in.
Base class for real dependency classes.

methods.name: denorm.dependencies.DependOnRelated.resolved_model
documentation:
Does all the initialization that had to wait until we knew which
model we depend on.

classes.name: denorm.dependencies.CallbackDependOnRelated

methods.name: denorm.dependencies.CallbackDependOnRelated.__init__
documentation:
Attaches a dependency to a callable, indicating the return value depends on
fields in an other model that is related to the model the callable belongs to
either through a ForeignKey in either direction or a ManyToManyField.

**Arguments:**

othermodel (required)

: Either a model class or a string naming a model class.

foreign_key

: The name of the ForeignKey or ManyToManyField that creates the relation
  between the two models.
  Only necessary if there is more than one relationship between the two models.

type

: One of 'forward', 'backward', 'forward_m2m' or 'backward_m2m'.
  If there are relations in both directions specify which one to use.

skip

: Use this to specify what fields change on every save().
  These fields will not be checked and will not make a model dirty when they change, t
  o prevent infinite loops.

only

: Use this to specify what fields should be watched instead of every field on save().
  Only those fields will be checked. Opposite of `skip`.

func

: Reference to function, that this is a callback to.

A DenormDependency that handles callbacks depending on fields
in other models that are related to the dependent model.

Two models are considered related if there is a ForeignKey or ManyToManyField
on either of them pointing to the other one.

modules.name: denorm.middleware
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: denorm.middleware.DenormMiddleware
documentation:
Calls `denorm.flush` during the response stage of every request. If your data mostly or only changes during
requests this should be a good idea. If you run into performance problems with this (because `flush()` takes
to long to complete) you can try using a background process or handle flushing manually instead.

As usual the order of middleware classes matters. It makes a lot of sense to put `DenormMiddleware`
after `TransactionMiddleware` in your `MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES` setting.
